symptoms.symptom_name: Anuria (~12 hours) days into induction chemotherapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Uric-acid nephropathy obstructs the renal tubules; anuria signals established AKI and the loss of potassium excretion.
CINV does not cause anuria — its presence forces the diagnosis beyond simple drug-induced vomiting.
Acute severe hypocalcaemia from hyperphosphataemia produces carpopedal spasm — a hallmark of the metabolic derangement in TLS.
Simple volume loss does not cause tetany; its presence points to a specific electrolyte catastrophe.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent forceful vomiting; vomited urate-lowering prophylaxis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Vomiting reflects uraemia/metabolic upset and means oral allopurinol is not being absorbed, so prophylaxis has failed.
Vomiting alone is compatible with CINV; only the accompanying anuria and tetany reveal the true severity.
Renal capsular distension from uric-acid crystal obstruction causes bilateral flank pain.
A calculus causes unilateral colic, not bilateral ache with systemic tetany and anuria.
Altered mentation reflects uraemia and electrolyte derangement and marks systemic decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Trousseau's sign and Chvostek's sign (clinician-elicited tetany signs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective bedside confirmation of severe hypocalcaemia — sealed clinician examination; the AI Doctor infers hypocalcaemia from the patient-reported claw-hand spasm, not from an elicited sign.
Renal capsular distension on palpation — clinician-only sign, sealed; not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia and hypertension on measured vitals (HR 118, BP 145/90)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-specific distress/metabolic stress response; sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor has no measured vitals in this encounter.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hyperkalaemia / hyperuricaemia / hyperphosphataemia / hypocalcaemia on serum panel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The defining laboratory tetrad of TLS with AKI — a hospital venous blood panel, never obtainable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Peaked T waves / arrhythmia changes on ECG

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG evidence of the immediate cardiac threat; requires equipment and continuous monitoring only available in hospital — sealed.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Anuria + tetany + relentless vomiting within days of starting chemotherapy for a bulky tumour is a recognise-and-escalate emergency pattern, even without any bloods.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent serum electrolytes, urea/creatinine, uric acid, phosphate and calcium (TLS panel)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines and grades TLS and quantifies the hyperkalaemia driving cardiac risk — must be done in hospital, not achievable via telehealth.
Detects hyperkalaemic changes and arrhythmia; the patient must be on a monitor during transfer and in the ED.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral rehydration solution advice as a management step
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Advising the anuric, vomiting, hyperkalaemic patient to drink fluids is unsafe and delays definitive care — an error of commission to avoid.
